clinical_history: This ECG from an elderly woman is most consistent with which ONE of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: The ECG shows sinus rhythm at a rate of about 80/min (P wave with a normal QRS axis and normal PR and QTc intervals. Relatively low voltage is present throughout, a nonspecific finding. The key abnormalities are the ST elevations of 1 to 2mm in leads I and aVL (with slight terminal T wave inversions) and ST depressions in III and aVF. The latter are consistent with reciprocal changes. Slow R wave progression is present in leads V1-V2, raising consideration of an anterior MI of indeterminate age. There is possible left atrial abnormality. The patient had an acute/evolving MI due to occlusion of a diagonal branch of the left anterior descending coronary artery. She underwent successful reperfusion with a percutaneous coronary intervention procedure.
